bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very poor for my preferences and gives no reliable sign of concession yet, so I will counter with my strongest valid values on every issue.

I targeted a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, and the opponent’s offers remain very poor for my preferences with only a tiny concession on issueA. To avoid simply repeating my last bid while making only a small early concession, I relaxed issueC from valueA to valueB, which is a relatively low-cost move for me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little need for major concessions yet. The opponent has only improved issueA from valueF to valueD while holding the other issues at valueF, so I am making a small concession on issueC while protecting my most important issueA and other high-value positions.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has only improved issueA from valueF toward valueC while holding the other issues at valueF, so their offers are still very low for me and do not justify conceding on my most important issueA. I avoid repeating my prior bid by making a small concession on issueC, where I have already shown flexibility.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession to avoid repeating my last offer. The opponent has been conceding only on issueA while holding issueB/issueC/issueD at valueF, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueA and issueB while conceding further on issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around 0.74 by keeping my most important issueA at valueA and preserving valueA on issueB and issueD. Since it is still early at t=0.10, I should not make broad concessions, but the opponent has steadily conceded issueA to valueA while holding issueB/issueC/issueD at valueF, so I will concede further on issueC to test whether that helps without giving up higher-priority ground.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, close to my previous concession level but not repeating the exact same bid. Since it is still early at t=0.12, I should not rush toward the opponent’s low offers; their bids show steady concession on issueA and a small move on issueB, while they remain firm on issueC/issueD at valueF. I will keep my strongest positions on issueA and issueB, but shift flexibility between issueC and issueD to test a tradeoff.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.71, since at t=0.14 there is still no need to concede too deeply. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me on issueA and issueB, while they have consistently held issueC and issueD at valueF, so I am conceding on issueD and partially on issueC while holding my most important gains on issueA and issueB.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while acknowledging the opponent has conceded on issueA and issueB but is still holding issueC and issueD at valueF. I will hold firm on issueA and issueB, concede issueD to valueF since it costs relatively little, and keep issueC at valueB rather than dropping to their valueF yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.71, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to concede down to the opponent’s latest offer yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and somewhat on issueB, but is holding firm on issueC and issueD at valueF; I will keep my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while allowing issueD=valueF and a moderate concession on issueC. This avoids repeating my immediately previous bid while staying close to bids the opponent has already seen.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is only a moderate concession from my best bids while still staying above the opponent’s latest offer to me. At t=0.20 there is still time, and the opponent’s offers show real concession on issueA, issueB, and recently issueC, while they have held issueD at valueF, so I concede issueD and press for a better issueC value.

I am targeting a utility around 0.738, which is still a strong outcome for me while making concessions from my ideal bid. At t=0.22 there is time left, so I should not fully collapse to the opponent’s last offer yet; their bids show real concession on issueA, issueB, and issueC, but they have held issueD at valueF, so I am testing a moderate move on issueD while keeping high-value terms elsewhere.

At t=0.24 there is still time, so I am targeting a strong but plausible utility rather than conceding to the opponent’s latest worse offer. The opponent has already offered issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueF, which gives me solid utility and avoids accepting their latest drop to issueD=valueE. Since they appear to have conceded substantially on A/B/C, I will hold those favorable values and only compromise on D.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, slightly above the opponent’s latest offer to me (~0.71) while still making a meaningful concession by leaving issueD at valueF. Time is still early-mid at t=0.26, so I do not need to accept their current level yet, but their history shows real concession on issueA and issueB and some movement on issueC while holding issueD low. I avoid repeating my exact previous bid and ask for a modest improvement on issueC while preserving the opponent-signaled issueD preference.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.71 by keeping my highest-priority gains on issueA and issueB while conceding on issueD, which the opponent has consistently held at valueF. At t=0.28 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest offer already gives me strong value and shows movement toward my preferences on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so matching that bid is a reasonable way to secure agreement without dropping too far.
